mechanism: Information routes through two separable processing systems: one accumulates associative pairings of stimuli and outcomes through repeated exposure (slow accrual); the other updates beliefs from explicitly presented symbolic information through conscious reasoning. When conflicting information arrives via each pathway, each system independently computes its representation and output, yielding independent evaluations of the same object.

An infant observes a sequence of events establishing object locations and actor goals. When object locations change while actor attention is diverted, the infant predicts actor behavior by representing the actor's outdated belief state, not the actual state. This capacity requires attributing a false proposition to another agent and using that attributed state to forecast action.

In rapid sequential visual presentation, two consecutive target-selection episodes interact: the first selection constrains the second through three dissociable mechanisms operating at different timescales. Selection efficacy (gate amplitude) recovers within 500ms. Selection latency (timing of engagement) remains delayed for over 800ms. Selection precision (temporal breadth) recovers within 400ms. Each reflects distinct neural processing stages responding to prior attentional engagement.

When attention focuses on a subset of items and ignores others, local item-specific features of ignored items degrade to near-chance noise. However, a pooling operation averages the noisy local signals across all ignored items, yielding accurate recovery of statistical summaries (centroid position). This pooling produces higher-fidelity abstract representations outside focus than inside for summary-level features.

Repeated estimates of a quantity from the same individual are sampled from an internal probability distribution, not selected deterministically from a fixed knowledge state. Averaging successive samples reduces error below either single sample due to noise independence. Temporal delay between samples increases independence (reduces anchoring), magnifying the benefit of averaging relative to immediate re-sampling.
distribution: independent random sampling from internal distribution

mechanism: Ambient temperature directly modulates abstract social-cognitive constructs through embodied mechanisms. Thermal sensations activate associated conceptual metaphors (warmth-affection linkage), shifting focus and updating representations. Warmer input induces concrete framing and relational processing; colder input induces abstract framing and property-focused processing. Effects propagate through language choice as a mediating pathway.
